_comment: Snippets for the Python script editor. Use snake_case APIs matching pm_bootstrap.py. RestrictedPython: use json_loads/json_dumps, b64encode, uuid_v4, datetime_now (no raw import json). See data/snippets/README.md.

categories._comment: Send sub-requests via pm.send_request. Useful from both pre-request and tests.
categories.name: Send requests

snippets.name: Send an HTTP request

categories._comment: Variable getters/setters/unsetters (snake_case). Visible in every script editor.
categories.name: Variables

categories._comment: Post-response assertions — same chains as JS via _Expectation (RestrictedPython-safe). Hidden from the pre-request editor.
categories.name: Tests

snippets.name: Response body: JSON value check
snippets.body:
def _t():
    json_data = pm.response.json()
    pm.expect(json_data["value"]).to.eql(100)
pm.test("Your test name", _t)

snippets.name: Response body: is equal to a string
Response time is less than 200ms
Status code: code name has string
Validate list of booleans (no jsonschema in sandbox)
snippets.body:
def _t():
    data = json_loads("[true, false]")
    pm.expect(isinstance(data, list)).to.be.true
    pm.expect(all(isinstance(x, bool) for x in data)).to.be.true
pm.test("Boolean array shape", _t)

snippets.name: Array length check
snippets.body:
def _t():
    arr = pm.response.json()["items"]
    pm.expect(arr).to.have.length_of(3)
pm.test("Returns three items", _t)

categories._comment: Pre-request setup (Python). Use json_loads/json_dumps, b64encode, uuid_v4, datetime_now. Hidden from the post-response editor.
categories.name: Request setup

snippets.name: Set basic auth header
snippets.body:
user = pm.environment.get("user")
pw = pm.environment.get("pass")
creds = b64encode(f"{user}:{pw}".encode()).decode()
pm.request.headers["Authorization"] = f"Basic {creds}"

snippets.name: Login flow: fetch token, save to env
snippets.body:
response = pm.send_request({
    "url": pm.environment.get("login_url"),
    "method": "POST",
    "headers": {"Content-Type": "application/json"},
    "body": json_dumps({"user": "u", "pass": "p"}),
})
body = response.get("body", "") if isinstance(response, dict) else ""
token = ""
if body:
    token = str(json_loads(body).get("token", ""))
pm.environment.set("token", token)

categories._comment: pm.require for PyPI packages. Pyodide runtime only; configure private mirrors at Settings → Private packages → PyPI. See docs/scripting/external-packages.md.
categories.name: Import PyPI packages

snippets.name: Import from public PyPI
snippets.body:
# Pyodide installs the package via micropip on first call.
# Pin exact versions for reproducibility.
jmespath = pm.require("jmespath==1.0.1")

body = pm.response.json()
matched = jmespath.search("items[?status=='active'].id", body)
print(matched)

snippets.name: Import from a private PyPI mirror
snippets.body:
# 1. Settings → Private packages → PyPI → Add PyPI index
#      Primary index URL: https://pypi.mycorp.io/simple/
#      Auth: Token (your CI token)
# 2. Apply.
# 3. pm.require resolves through the configured mirror, with auth
#    URL-embedded for micropip.
internal_sdk = pm.require("internal-sdk==1.2.0")
client = internal_sdk.Client(base_url=pm.environment.get("api"))
print(client.ping())

snippets.name: Import several pinned packages
snippets.body:
# Each pm.require call triggers a separate micropip.install at run
# time. Pin versions to keep test results reproducible.
requests = pm.require("requests==2.31.0")
jwt = pm.require("PyJWT==2.8.0")
yaml = pm.require("pyyaml==6.0.1")

token = jwt.encode({"sub": "alice"}, "secret", algorithm="HS256")
print(token)

snippets.name: Decode a JWT response with PyJWT
Validate response with pydantic
snippets.body:
pydantic = pm.require("pydantic==2.6.0")

class User(pydantic.BaseModel):
    id: int
    email: str

def check_user():
    body = pm.response.json()
    user = User.model_validate(body)
    pm.expect(user.email).to.include("@")

pm.test("Response matches User shape", check_user)
